Klasse SudokuFitnessFunction

java.lang.Object
de.fhdwhannover.integrea.ga.problems.sudoku.SudokuFitnessFunction
Alle implementierten Schnittstellen:
FitnessFunction<Integer>
public class SudokuFitnessFunction extends Object implements FitnessFunction<Integer>
Fitness Function for evaluating Sudoku grids.

A standard 9x9 Sudoku puzzle is solved when every row, column, and 3x3 sub-grid contains the digits 1 through 9 exactly once without repetition.

This function calculates fitness by summing the number of unique values present in every row (max 9x9=81), column (max 9x9=81), and 3x3 block (max 9x9=81). Therefore, a perfectly valid Sudoku grid achieves a maximum fitness score of 243.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • SudokuFitnessFunction

      public SudokuFitnessFunction(int[][] initialGrid)

  • Methodendetails

    • evaluate

      public int evaluate(Individual<Integer> individual)
      Evaluates the fitness of a specific Sudoku grid.

      The fitness value represents the sum of the number of unique digits found in every row, column, and 3x3 block. This value is not scaled and has a strict mathematical maximum of 243. The lower the number of duplicates, the higher the fitness.

      Angegeben von:
      evaluate in Schnittstelle FitnessFunction<Integer>
      Parameter:
      individual - The candidate Sudoku grid.
      Gibt zurück:
      The calculated fitness value between 0 and 243.
